name: Doi Inthanon National Park
description: Doi Inthanon is the highest mountain in Thailand at 2,565 meters above sea level. The national park features spectacular waterfalls such as Mae Ya Waterfall and Wachirathan Waterfall, misty forests, and diverse wildlife. Visitors can see rhododendron flowers blooming from November to February. Two royal chedis dedicated to the King and Queen are located at the summit. Over 380 bird species are recorded.
highlights: Highest peak in Thailand, royal chedis, waterfalls, bird watching, cool climate

name: Wat Phra That Doi Suthep
description: Wat Phra That Doi Suthep is one of Thailand's most sacred Buddhist temples, perched 1,073 meters above sea level on Doi Suthep mountain overlooking Chiang Mai city. The golden chedi is a magnificent landmark visible from the city. A white elephant carried a sacred relic up the mountain and died on this spot according to legend. Visitors climb 306 steps flanked by Naga serpents or take the cable car.
highlights: Golden chedi, panoramic Chiang Mai views, Naga staircase, sacred Buddhist relic

name: White Temple (Wat Rong Khun)
description: Wat Rong Khun, known as the White Temple, is a contemporary Buddhist temple designed by artist Chalermchai Kositpipat. The structure is covered in white plaster embedded with mirror glass, symbolizing purity. The bridge of rebirth crosses a sea of hands reaching upward, symbolizing those trapped in suffering. The interior features murals mixing pop culture characters with Buddhist themes.
highlights: Unique white architecture, mirror glass art, symbolic bridge, pop art murals

name: Blue Temple (Wat Rong Suea Ten)
description: Wat Rong Suea Ten, the Blue Temple, is a stunning azure-colored temple in Chiang Rai completed in 2016. The name means Temple of the Dancing Tiger. Vivid cobalt blue hues combined with gold accents and intricate dragon carvings make it one of the most photogenic temples in Thailand. A large white Buddha sits at the center. The temple is free to enter and less crowded than the White Temple.
highlights: Vibrant blue architecture, white Buddha, dragon sculptures

name: Golden Triangle
description: The Golden Triangle is where the borders of Thailand, Laos, and Myanmar meet at the confluence of the Ruak and Mekong rivers. Once notorious as one of the largest opium-producing regions, today it is a major tourist destination. Visitors can see all three countries from one spot, take boat trips on the Mekong, and visit the House of Opium museum. The area offers stunning riverside scenery.
highlights: Three-country viewpoint, Mekong River, opium museum, boat trips

name: Grand Palace and Wat Phra Kaew
description: The Grand Palace is the most iconic landmark in Bangkok, serving as the official royal residence since 1782. Within its walls lies Wat Phra Kaew (Temple of the Emerald Buddha), home to the most revered image in Thai Buddhism. The complex covers 218,000 square meters with hundreds of ornate buildings blending Thai and European Baroque architecture. Visitors must dress modestly with covered shoulders and legs.
highlights: Emerald Buddha, royal buildings, intricate Thai architecture
nearby: Wat Arun, Wat Pho, Chao Phraya River

name: Wat Arun (Temple of Dawn)
description: Wat Arun stands on the west bank of the Chao Phraya River. Its central prang rises 79 meters and is decorated with Chinese porcelain fragments and colorful ceramics. The temple is dedicated to Aruna, the Hindu god of dawn. It is particularly stunning at sunrise and when illuminated at night. Visitors can climb the steep prang steps for panoramic river views.
highlights: Prang tower, Chao Phraya views, porcelain mosaic, sunrise scenery

name: Chatuchak Weekend Market
description: Chatuchak Weekend Market is one of the world's largest markets, covering 35 acres with more than 8,000 stalls and 200,000 visitors every weekend. Open only Saturday and Sunday, it sells antiques, handicrafts, clothing, plants, pets, street food, and art. The market is organized into 27 sections. It is a treasure hunter's paradise and one of the best places to experience authentic Thai shopping culture.
highlights: 8,000+ stalls, antiques, street food, Thai handicrafts

name: Ayutthaya Historical Park
description: Ayutthaya was the second capital of the Siamese Kingdom and one of Asia's most prosperous cities during the 14th-18th centuries. The UNESCO World Heritage park contains temple ruins, monasteries, and statues. Notable sites include Wat Mahathat with its famous Buddha head entwined in tree roots, and Wat Phra Si Sanphet with three iconic chedis. The park is best explored by bicycle.
highlights: UNESCO ruins, Buddha head in tree roots, ancient chedis
nearby: Bangkok (80 km), Bang Pa-In Royal Palace

name: Floating Markets of Bangkok
description: Thailand's floating markets are iconic experiences where vendors sell food, fruits, and goods from wooden boats on waterways. Amphawa Floating Market (open weekends) is known for evening firefly boat tours and fresh seafood. Damnoen Saduak is the most famous and photogenic. Taling Chan near Bangkok is local and relaxed. These markets offer unique Thai cuisine including boat noodles and grilled seafood.
highlights: Boat vendors, fresh local food, canal boat tours, Thai atmosphere
entrance_fee: Free (pay for food and boat rides)

name: Koh Chang
description: Koh Chang is Thailand's second-largest island, offering pristine beaches, tropical rainforest, waterfalls, and coral reefs. White Sand Beach is the most developed. Lonely Beach is popular with backpackers. Klong Plu Waterfall is reachable by a short jungle trek. Mountains cover 70% of the land, keeping it natural. Snorkeling and diving around nearby smaller islands is excellent.
highlights: Pristine beaches, jungle waterfalls, snorkeling, rainforest trekking

name: Pattaya
description: Pattaya is Thailand's most famous beach resort city on the Gulf of Thailand, 145 km south of Bangkok. Attractions include Nong Nooch Tropical Botanical Garden, the Sanctuary of Truth (a massive wooden temple), and Koh Larn island with crystal waters. Walking Street has entertainment, shows, and restaurants. The city has transformed from a fishing village to a major tourist hub.
highlights: Sanctuary of Truth, Koh Larn island, botanical garden, beach activities

name: Phi Phi Islands
description: The Phi Phi Islands are among the most beautiful in Southeast Asia. Phi Phi Leh features Maya Bay, made famous by the movie The Beach, surrounded by limestone cliffs. The waters are crystal-clear and ideal for snorkeling and diving. Viking Cave contains prehistoric paintings. Phi Phi Don has a lively village with hostels and restaurants.
highlights: Maya Bay, limestone cliffs, diving, snorkeling, island hopping

name: Phuket
description: Phuket is Thailand's largest island and premier beach destination. Patong Beach is the most vibrant with water sports and nightlife. Kata and Karon are family-friendly. The historic Sino-Portuguese Phuket Old Town, the Big Buddha (45m tall), Wat Chalong, and elephant sanctuaries offer cultural depth. Phang Nga Bay reveals dramatic limestone islands including James Bond Island. World-class dining and luxury resorts complete the experience.
highlights: Patong Beach, Big Buddha, Phang Nga Bay, Old Town architecture
nearby: Phi Phi Islands, Similan Islands, Krabi

name: Koh Samui
description: Koh Samui is Thailand's third-largest island in the Gulf of Thailand. Chaweng Beach is the most developed and lively. Lamai Beach is more relaxed. The iconic Big Buddha statue (Wat Phra Yai) sits on a small islet. Na Muang waterfalls offer jungle adventure. The Ang Thong Marine National Park nearby features 42 islands with hidden lagoons. Samui has a vibrant food scene and regular festivals.
highlights: Chaweng Beach, Big Buddha statue, Ang Thong Marine Park, luxury resorts
nearby: Koh Phangan (Full Moon Party), Koh Tao (diving)

name: Railay Beach
description: Railay Beach is a peninsula only accessible by boat due to high limestone cliffs. This car-free paradise has four beaches: Railay West for swimming and sunsets, Railay East with mangroves, Phra Nang Beach (one of Thailand's most beautiful) with a princess cave shrine, and Tonsai Beach for rock climbers. The karst formations offer hundreds of climbing routes. Snorkeling and kayaking are also popular.
highlights: Phra Nang Cave, rock climbing, boat-access only, limestone karsts
nearby: Krabi Town, Ao Nang, Phi Phi Islands

name: Similan Islands
description: The Similan Islands National Marine Park is one of the world's top diving and snorkeling destinations. The archipelago features translucent turquoise water, vibrant coral reefs, and diverse marine life including whale sharks, manta rays, sea turtles, and reef fish. The park is closed May to October for conservation. Smooth granite boulders, white sand beaches, and jungle complete the experience.
highlights: World-class diving, manta rays, whale sharks, coral reefs

name: Khao Sok National Park
description: Khao Sok National Park contains one of the world's most ancient rainforests. The dramatic Cheow Lan Lake features floating bungalows surrounded by jungle-clad limestone cliffs. The park is home to Rafflesia flowers (world's largest), Asian elephants, tigers, and tapirs. Night safaris, kayaking, and jungle trekking are popular. Morning mist over the lake with towering limestone peaks is unforgettable.
highlights: Floating bungalows, limestone karsts, jungle trekking, Rafflesia flowers

name: Phanom Rung Historical Park
description: Phanom Rung is Thailand's finest Khmer temple complex, built between the 10th and 13th centuries on an extinct volcano 402 meters above the Korat Plateau. The temple was dedicated to Shiva. Four times a year, sunlight aligns perfectly through all 15 doorways of the temple. The processional way is flanked by 69 sandstone posts and four naga bridges. The restoration project took over 17 years.
highlights: Khmer architecture, solar alignment event, volcanic hill setting

name: Nong Khai and Sala Kaew Ku
description: Nong Khai is a riverside city on the Mekong River bordering Laos. Sala Kaew Ku is a bizarre sculpture park created by mystic shaman Luang Pu Bunleua Sulilat, containing enormous concrete sculptures depicting Hindu-Buddhist mythology. Some sculptures stand over 25 meters tall. The Friendship Bridge connects Thailand to Vientiane, Laos. The riverfront is perfect for sunset watching and river fish dining.
highlights: Bizarre sculpture park, Mekong River, Laos border crossing

name: Erawan National Park
description: Erawan National Park is famous for its emerald-green seven-tiered waterfall, one of the most beautiful in Thailand. Each level forms turquoise pools for swimming among limestone formations. Blue-grey fish nibble visitors' feet in the pools. The name Erawan comes from the three-headed white elephant of Hindu mythology. The full hike to level 7 takes 2-3 hours through lush jungle.
highlights: Seven-tiered waterfall, turquoise pools, swimming, jungle trekking

name: River Kwai Bridge and Kanchanaburi
description: The Bridge on the River Kwai is a famous WWII historical site. The steel bridge was built by Allied prisoners of war under Japanese military to create the Death Railway. The JEATH War Museum and Allied War Cemetery memorialize the 100,000+ laborers who died during construction. A scenic Death Railway train ride runs through the mountains. Kanchanaburi sits in a beautiful valley with rivers and forests.
highlights: WWII bridge, death railway train ride, war museums, Allied Cemetery

name: Khao Yai National Park
description: Khao Yai is one of Asia's finest national parks and a UNESCO World Heritage Site, covering 2,168 sq km. The park is home to Asian elephants, Indochinese tigers, gaurs, hornbills, gibbons, and pythons. Haew Narok waterfall drops 150 meters. Night safaris frequently spot deer and civets. The surrounding area has luxury resorts and the PB Valley Khao Yai Winery.
highlights: Asian elephants, tigers, hornbills, Haew Narok waterfall, night safari

name: Pai
description: Pai is a bohemian small town in the mountains of Mae Hong Son province, loved by backpackers, yogis, and artists. Set in a lush valley, the town blends Thai, Chinese Yunnan, and hippie influences. Attractions include Pai Canyon, Yun Lai Viewpoint for sea of fog, Santichon Chinese Village, hot springs, and a memorial bridge. The town is famous for its night market, live music, craft coffee, and yoga retreats.
highlights: Pai Canyon, sea of fog, hot springs, bohemian atmosphere, night market
nearby: Mae Hong Son, Chiang Mai (3 hours)

name: Sukhothai Historical Park
description: Sukhothai was the first capital of the Thai Kingdom, founded in 1238 AD. The UNESCO World Heritage park contains ruins of 193 temples across 70 sq km. Key highlights include Wat Mahathat (the spiritual center), Wat Si Chum with its enormous 15m seated Buddha, and Wat Saphan Hin. Best explored by bicycle. The annual Loy Krathong festival originated here and is celebrated with lanterns, candles, and fireworks every November.
best_season: October to February (Loy Krathong in November)
highlights: First Thai capital, UNESCO ruins, giant Buddha, Loy Krathong origin
